key: # --- Article 1: "How Tech's Biggest Companies Are Offloading the Risks of the A.I. Boom" (NYT

detail: Meta secured ~$30B via Blue Owl Capital / Beignet Investor LLC SPV for Louisiana data center; Blue Owl on hook for 80% of financing
Meta agreed to rent the Beignet/Hyperion data center from SPV with series of 4-year leases; classified as operating cost not debt
Meta can walk away from deal as early as 2033; bond maturity is 2049 — 16-year risk gap between exit option and bond holders
Beignet bonds sold by Pimco to insurers/pension funds/endowments/financial advisers; mature 2049; BlackRock also purchased
Microsoft $17B deal with Nebius (neocloud, ex-Yandex founder) signed Sep 2025
Microsoft $23B deal with Nscale (privately-held British neocloud) signed Oct 2025; figure per Bloomberg
Microsoft $10B deal with Iren (former bitcoin miner) signed Nov 2025
Microsoft signed $50B+ in neocloud leases (Nebius+Nscale+Iren+Lambda) in Sep-Nov 2025; 3-5 year terms; shows as opex not capex
OpenAI committed to buy up to $22.4B in computing power directly from CoreWeave
CoreWeave borrowing billions at 10%+ interest rates; future tied to OpenAI demand
OpenAI has promised to send $250B in computing Microsoft's way

key: # --- Article 2: "What Exactly Are A.I. Companies Trying to Build?" (NYT

detail: Amazon+Microsoft+Google+Meta+OpenAI plan to spend $325B+ combined by end of 2025 on AI pursuit
ChatGPT has 700M+ monthly users
Paid subscribers account for fewer than 6% of ChatGPT users; free version still unprofitable
Google search generates $54B quarterly ad revenue; ~2B daily users
~80% of businesses started using gen AI but equally many said no significant bottom-line impact (McKinsey research)
Andy Jassy: ~10% of AI infra used for building AI; 80-90% used to deliver AI to customers
Meta found people ~7% more likely to click on ads created with new AI techniques
Musk/xAI offering AI companion bots via subscription at $300/month
Project Rainier: 1200 acres of former cornfield near New Carlisle IN
Project Rainier will consume 2.2 GW of electricity — enough to power a million homes
Amazon plans ~30 data centers at site; 7 built by early June 2025; each larger than a football stadium
Seven data centers constructed as of early June 2025; bulldozers on 8th; parking lot becomes 9th
Amazon has invested $8B in Anthropic; Rainier built with single customer (Anthropic) in mind
Indiana 50-year sales tax break worth ~$4B (Citizens Action Coalition estimate)
County property and technology tax breaks could save Amazon additional ~$4B over 35 years
In tax deal Amazon committed $11B for 16 buildings; now plans nearly double (~30)
AEP Indiana peak power: 2.8 GW in 2024; data centers will push to 7+ GW by ~2030
AEP projects 7+ GW peak by ~2030; Amazon campus = ~half of additional load growth
AEP told regulators it expects natural gas plants to provide ~75% of additional power needed by 2030
~4000 construction workers on site on typical week
Amazon paid $120K for traffic enforcement overtime + $7M for road improvements
Dewatering permit: 2.2M gallons/hour for 730 days; state investigating dry neighbor wells
Amazon acquired Annapurna Labs (Israeli chip designer) in 2015; started AI chip design 2016
Meta building 2-GW data center in Louisiana (cross-referenced with risk offloading article)
OpenAI erecting 1.2-GW facility in Texas and nearly as large in UAE
